item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed the imagery of objects being created and the ejection burning mechanisms, but I can’t help but feeling this hasn’t met its full potential. The historical addenda is fun but this is dying for some kind of test log or incident report that really lays out how nuts this thing could get with imaginative writing/object combinations.

Maybe that’s explored more in the GoI format, but as it stands here I no-vote this. It’s a tough no vote for me because I absolutely love the concept and there’s really nothing wrong with how you put this together but there’s a void in this article that I am not able to overlook.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So this is why Foundation personnel are so unusually loyal…

+1, your combination of Factory and Spirit was truly inspired.

item.title: Re: "This here's quality Author Commentary. Smuggled straight down from Canada."
item.content:encoded:
IHP was a blast to work with, 10/10 would collab again.

I would like to thank Captain Kirby and Papa Sen for offering critique on this piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This deeply frightens me and my socialist tendencies. +1 duly awarded.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Using the primary effect got a "nice" from me. Using the secondary effect too (even though I knew it had to happen) got a big oof.

item.title: "This here's quality Author Commentary. Smuggled straight down from Canada."
item.content:encoded:
This is the second part of Uncle Nicolini and mine's CollabCon project, featuring the Chicago Spirit and the Factory. Nico was a joy to work with (and incredibly patient, given my sleep schedule or lack thereof) and I had fun writing this.

Bit of trivia with our collab con thing: we were originally planning on going for a UIU/Wilson's Wildlife collaboration, with the two of them working together to raid a critter fighting ring and coming across a war elephant so badly damaged that they had no choice to turn it over to the Foundation. But then word came through that someone else was doing Wilsons x UIU, and we decided to change it to the Factory x CS.

I said a long, long while back— all the way back when the Chicago Spirit hub was originally a tale about a user that vanished from existence— that I'd write something for them. It only took me almost five goddamn years.

So, yeah. Enjoy!

Image Pesto: http://archives.seattle.gov/digital-collections/index.php/Detail/objects/128206
